steps.title: Read Fault Codes
steps.instruction: Connect an OBD-II scanner to the port under the dashboard (driver's side). Read all stored and pending DTCs. Note all codes before clearing. A solid CEL is non-urgent; a flashing CEL indicates an active misfire — do not drive at highway speed.
tools: OBD-II scanner or Bluetooth dongle + app
steps.decision: Are there any P0 (powertrain) codes stored?

steps.title: Research Code Priority
steps.instruction: Identify the primary code (first stored code). Group codes: fuel system (P01xx), ignition (P03xx), catalyst/emissions (P04xx), oxygen sensors (P013x-P015x). Address primary code first — secondary codes often clear once root cause is fixed.
steps.decision: Is the primary code related to oxygen sensor or fuel system?

steps.title: Inspect Oxygen Sensors
steps.instruction: With the engine warm, use a live data scanner to monitor O2 sensor waveforms. Upstream sensor should oscillate 0.1-0.9V rapidly. A flat waveform or stuck voltage indicates a failed sensor.
steps.decision: Is the upstream or downstream O2 sensor waveform abnormal?

steps.title: Inspect MAF Sensor and Vacuum Lines
steps.instruction: Check the MAF sensor reading at idle (should be ~2-7 g/s for most 4-cyl engines). Inspect all vacuum hoses for cracks, disconnections, or collapse. Spray carburetor cleaner around intake manifold gaskets to detect vacuum leaks.
steps.decision: Is MAF reading out of spec or are vacuum leaks detected?

decision_points.question: Flashing CEL (active misfire)?
decision_points.yes: Do not drive — diagnose immediately; P0300 misfires

decision_points.question: O2 sensor code with flat waveform?

resolution_paths.condition: Faulty oxygen sensor confirmed by waveform or code

resolution_paths.action: Replace intake manifold gasket or repair vacuum hose

steps.title: Check Hydraulic Fluid Level and Condition
steps.instruction: With the machine on level ground and all cylinders retracted, check the hydraulic tank sight glass. Level should be at or above the MIN mark. Check fluid color: milky = water contamination; dark/burnt smell = overheating degradation.
steps.decision: Is fluid level low or fluid condition degraded?

steps.title: Inspect for External Leaks
steps.instruction: Walk around the machine and inspect all hydraulic hoses, cylinders, and fittings. Look for wet areas, fluid trails, or pooling under the machine. Clean suspected areas with rags and cycle the circuit to identify active leak points.
steps.decision: Is an external leak source visible?

steps.title: Measure System Pressure
steps.instruction: Connect a hydraulic pressure gauge at the main relief valve port. Start the engine and measure standby pressure (typically 200-350 bar depending on machine). Then stall the circuit against a solid load and measure relief pressure. Compare to service manual specs.
steps.decision: Is main relief pressure below specification?

steps.title: Cylinder Drift Test
steps.instruction: Lift a full bucket load and raise the boom. Shut off the engine. Observe for cylinder drift over 5 minutes. Drift indicates internal cylinder seal leak or control valve leak. Identify which cylinder is drifting.
steps.decision: Is there measurable cylinder drift after engine shutdown?

decision_points.question: Fluid low or contaminated?
decision_points.yes: Top up/change fluid and inspect for source

decision_points.question: Relief pressure below spec?
decision_points.yes: Adjust or replace main relief valve

resolution_paths.action: Adjust or replace main relief valve

steps.title: Check Battery and Starter Circuit
steps.instruction: Test battery voltage with a multimeter — should be 12.4V or above at rest. Test voltage during cranking — should not drop below 9.5V. Check battery terminals for corrosion. Verify the E-Stop (emergency stop) button is released.
steps.decision: Is battery voltage above 12.4V and cranking voltage above 9.5V?

steps.title: Check Fuel System
steps.instruction: Verify fuel tank is at least 1/4 full. Check fuel shutoff valve is open. Inspect the fuel filter for clogging (replace if not changed in last 500 hours). Check fuel lines for kinks or cracks. For diesel, check for air in fuel lines (bleed if needed).
steps.decision: Is there adequate clean fuel reaching the injection system?

steps.title: Inspect Air Intake and Filter
steps.instruction: Remove and inspect the air filter. A clogged filter causes hard starting and black smoke. Check the intake for obstructions (bird nests are common in outdoor generators).
steps.decision: Is the air filter heavily clogged or intake obstructed?

steps.title: Check Governor and Throttle
steps.instruction: Inspect the throttle linkage for binding or damage. Verify the governor arm moves freely. Check for fault codes on the generator control module if equipped with digital controls.
steps.decision: Is the throttle/governor mechanism binding or are control module faults present?

decision_points.question: Battery/starting circuit issue?
decision_points.yes: Charge or replace battery / repair starter

decision_points.question: Fuel starvation confirmed?
decision_points.yes: Replace fuel filter / bleed fuel lines

resolution_paths.condition: Clogged fuel filter or air in diesel fuel
resolution_paths.action: Replace fuel filter and bleed fuel system
Repair or replace generator control panel

steps.title: Compare Output to Baseline
steps.instruction: Review inverter monitoring data or app. Compare current daily output (kWh) to the same period in prior years and to the manufacturer's performance ratio. A drop over 20% vs. baseline warrants investigation.
steps.decision: Is output more than 20% below historical baseline for this season?

steps.title: Inspect Panels for Shading and Soiling
steps.instruction: Visually inspect all panels from ground level with binoculars or drone. Look for: bird droppings, pollen/dust buildup, leaves, nearby tree growth causing new shading, or physical damage (cracks, delamination, discoloration).
steps.decision: Is soiling, shading, or physical damage observed on any panel?

steps.title: Check Inverter Faults and String Voltages
steps.instruction: Access the inverter display or monitoring portal. Check for fault codes or alarm logs. Use a multimeter to measure open-circuit voltage (Voc) at each string input on the inverter. Compare measured Voc to spec (typically: panels_in_string × Voc_per_panel × 0.85 for temperature correction).
steps.decision: Is any string voltage significantly below expected value?

steps.title: Isolate Underperforming Panels
steps.instruction: Use a clamp meter to measure current at each panel or use a thermal camera to identify hotspots (failed cells appear as hot spots). An IV curve tracer gives the most precise panel-level data.
steps.decision: Is one or more panels producing significantly less current?

decision_points.question: String voltage low on one string?
decision_points.yes: Isolate individual panels in that string

resolution_paths.condition: Faulty panel with hotspot or failed cell string
Inverter fault code or internal failure
resolution_paths.action: Reset or replace inverter / contact manufacturer
